exemplar.title: How does Gabriel Garcia creatively use characters to examine the detrimental effects of gender and power issues on women in his text, Chronicles of a Death Foretold?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Gabriel Garcia creatively use characters to examine the detrimental effects of gender and power issues on women in his text, Chronicles of a Death Foretold?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Consistent demonstration of knowledge of text and context (e.g., patriarchal structures, honour culture).
Sustained interpretation linking gender expectations to economic, social, and physical violence.
Effective use of well-chosen quotations with page references to support main arguments.
weaknesses: Occasional over-generalizations (e.g., âeach societyâ¦has its gender rolesâ) dilute analytical precision.
Secondary source (Kitchin & Thrift) is tangential and not fully integrated.
Interpretations are asserted rather than explored in depth, limiting persuasiveness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay clearly demonstrates good knowledge and understanding of the work, with sustained interpretation of its implications. References to key plot events and quotations are effectively chosen and support the thesis. Minor generalizations and an under-integrated secondary source prevent the work from reaching an âexcellentâ band, so a score of 4 accurately reflects its quality.

strengths: Identification of relevant textual features (diction, imagery, hyperbole) linked to patriarchal power.
Functional analytical lens that moves beyond mere summary in several passages.
Some insightful notes on symbolism (e.g., wealth as male dominance).
weaknesses: Analysis often moves too quickly from feature to societal conclusion without unpacking effect and purpose.
Reliance on descriptive statements and moral reactions in places.
Depth of evaluation is intermittent; lacks the consistent insight required for a higher band.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate provides generally appropriate analysis and evaluation of authorial choices, showing a functional understanding of technique and its social significance. However, analysis is not consistently probing or insightful, and at times slips into description. This aligns with the band-3 descriptor, meriting a score of 3.

strengths: Clear overall structure with a defined line of inquiry (introduction, body sections, conclusion).
Effective use of topic sentences and signposting to guide the reader.
Logical progression of ideas tying back to the patriarchal theme.
weaknesses: Some repetition of ideas (âpatriarchal society,â âcommodify womenâ) weakens cohesion.
Occasional digressions into broad cultural commentary.
Supporting examples are sometimes dropped as standalone quotations rather than fully integrated into syntax.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization of ideas is sound and mostly cohesive, and the line of inquiry is maintained throughout. However, mechanical integration of quotations and some repetitive or digressive passages mean that supporting examples are only sometimes well integrated. The overall performance fits the band-3 descriptor for Focus, Organization and Development, justifying a score of 3.

strengths: Meaning is generally clear and academic vocabulary is appropriate for literary analysis.
Overall register is formal and thesis-driven in many sections.
weaknesses: Frequent grammar, punctuation, and sentence-construction errors (e.g., missing commas, tense inconsistencies).
Mechanical parenthetical citations and inconsistent formatting detract from academic fluency.
Occasional lapses into colloquial phrasing and emotive language undercut formal register.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the essay communicates meaning, the cumulative effect of noticeable lapses in grammar, punctuation, and register, as well as mechanical citation style, reduces overall clarity and precision. These features align more closely with the band-2 descriptor for Language, resulting in a score of 2.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Supporting examples are **sometimes well integrated**âquotes are introduced and followed by explanationâbut several instances place quotations as stand-alone sentences or repeat citation details awkwardly (â(GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquez, 2014, 26) The hyperboleâ¦â).

The student regularly provides interpretative comments after evidence, yet the mechanical parenthetical references and uneven embedding indicate only partial mastery of integration conventions.

This mixed performance aligns with **band 3**, where integration is âsometimesâ rather than âmostlyâ well executed.

The essay follows a clear structure: introductory LoI and thesis, three body sections each centred on a character, and a synthesising conclusion. Topic sentences forecast points, and internal signposting (âFirstly,â âAdditionally,â âFurthermoreâ) guides readers, creating a **well-organised, mostly cohesive** line of inquiry.

Development is adequate; each paragraph returns to the patriarchal theme and ties back to the thesis, showing logical progression. Occasional repetition of ideas (âpatriarchal society,â âcommodify womenâ) and some digressions into general cultural commentary slightly weaken cohesion but do not disrupt overall flow.

Given its solid architecture and mostly sustained argument, the work fits comfortably in **band 4** rather than the fully polished, âwell-developedâ standard of band 5.

The essay supplies **relevant quotations** for every main paragraph, usually accompanied by page numbers and brief discussion (e.g., âBayardoâ¦ âswimming in goldâ,â âHe grabbed my whole pussyâ¦â). These excerpts clearly support the gender-power argument and are not merely decorative.

Most quotations are integrated grammatically, and follow-up explanation links them to the thesis. The support is therefore **effective**, enabling the reader to see evidence for each claim.

Minor weaknessesâoccasional block quotations dropped without full syntactic integration and one secondary reference (Kitchin & Thrift) of doubtful relevanceâprevent the work from reaching the sophistication expected for band 5, but it solidly fulfils band 4 criteria.

The register is predominantly formal and academic, with thesis-driven prose and discipline-specific vocabulary (âpatriarchal mindset,â âcommodifyâ). Nonetheless, colloquial touches (âpussy,â âus,â âswimming in goldâ) and direct reader address slightly dilute consistency.

Style generally matches an HL essay but lacks the refined precision that would characterise band 4 or 5. Occasional phrasing is verbose or repetitive, signalling adequate rather than exemplary control.

These features fulfil the descriptor âregister and style mostly appropriate to the task,â positioning the work in **band 3**.

Overall **clarity is acceptable**: sentences communicate meaning and vocabulary is appropriate for literary analysis. However, noticeable lapses (âwhich seemed as her way of escaping reality,â missing commas, inconsistent tense) occur regularly, and verbâsubject agreement falters at points.

Despite these issues, meaning remains clear; grammar and lexis are sufficiently accurate to avoid confusion. This corresponds to **band 3**, where language is clear âdespite some lapses,â rather than the more secure accuracy expected in band 4.

The student identifies textual featuresâdiction (âdeliriousâ), imagery (âswimming in goldâ), hyperbole, explicit languageâand links them to patriarchal power, showing **appropriate** but not consistently probing analysis. Commentary often moves quickly from feature to social conclusion without unpacking technique, effect, and purpose in depth.

Some remarks slide into description (âThis makes the readers reflectâ¦â) or moral reaction, signalling reliance on narrative summary. Yet the discussion of power symbolism and narrative perspective demonstrates a functional analytical lens beyond mere paraphrase.

Because insight is intermittent and evaluative depth limited, the work aligns with the descriptor âgenerally appropriate analysisâ in **band 3**, not yet reaching the âinsightfulâ consistency of band 4.

annotations.comment: Several sentences in the conclusion are wordy. Aim for conciseness by removing redundant phrases.
The line of inquiry effectively frames the topic but could be more specific about which aspects of gender and power will be examined. Consider narrowing the scope to guide the analysis more sharply.
The thesis articulates the central claim but remains broad. Strengthen it by specifying the âdetrimental effectsâ you will analyze for each character.

The introduction is densely packed and includes a secondary source that isnât clearly integrated. Consider splitting this into two paragraphs and removing or better justifying the Kitchin & Thrift reference.
The paragraph restates the thesis almost verbatim at the end, which feels repetitive. Aim to synthesize rather than repeat your claim here.
Sentence trails off abruptly at the start of the Bayardo section. Ensure each sentence is complete and clearly conveys a single idea.
The passive phrasing in the opening sentence (âserves as a prismâ) reduces impact. Consider an active construction to engage the reader more effectively.
The quote about âthe huge weight of his power and fortuneâ is well chosen. It effectively illustrates how wealth functions as a symbol of male dominance.
Several quotations appear as standalone sentences; integrating them into your own syntax will improve cohesion (e.g., introduce the quote within your sentence).
The analysis leaps quickly from diction to societal conclusion without unpacking how âdeliriousâ shapes the readerâs perception. Unpack the effect of the term step by step.
The parenthetical citation style is somewhat mechanical. Use signal phrases (e.g., âAs MÃ¡rquez writes (26)â¦â) to maintain academic fluency.
Your discussion of imagery and violent diction in Divinaâs section shows a good grasp of authorial choices. Continue this close focus on technique.
The section describing Divina includes emotive language (âus,â âour strong emotionsâ) that undercuts academic distance. Maintain formal register consistently.
Missing comma before the clause âwhich seemed as her way of escaping reality.â Proofread punctuation to ensure clarity.
The transition to Angelaâs analysis accurately signals the shift in focus. Consider linking this more explicitly back to your thesis to reinforce cohesion.
Typographical error in the heading (âDeath Forefoldâ instead of âForetoldâ). Correcting such errors enhances professionalism.
The quote on Angelaâs despair is powerful and well chosen; it vividly captures the stakes of virginity in patriarchal culture.
The concluding sentence of this paragraph introduces a broad generalization about all societies. Focus on textual analysis rather than sweeping cultural claims.
This vocabulary (âkill myself,â âdistressedâ) effectively conveys Angelaâs psychological turmoil. Strong focus on diction enhances your interpretation.
The conclusion reiterates key ideas but is overly repetitive. Try condensing to highlight new insights rather than restating arguments.
The concluding call to challenge patriarchal views provides a strong sense of purpose and closure.
The references list correctly cites primary text but the Kitchin & Thrift entry lacks consistency in formatting. Ensure uniform style.
Is the International Encyclopedia of Human Geography reference directly relevant to your literary analysis? Consider removing tangential sources.
